def: Without sudden change of direction or curvature; smooth;fowing; -- said of the figure of a vessel, and of surfaces, waterlines, and other lines.
The ordination of a person to fill a station already occupied;especially, the ordination by an ecclesiastical official, during hislifetime, of his successor. Fuller.
The beaked whale (Balænoptera rostrata), from which doeglingoil is obtained.
Wide and general destruction; devastation; waste.As for Saul, he made havoc of the church. Acts viii. 3.Ye gods, what havoc does ambition make Among your works! Addison.
One of the first three Gospels of the New Testament. SeeSynoptist.
Goddess of the seasons, or of the hours of the day.Lo! where the rosy-blosomed Hours, Fair Venus' train, appear. Gray.
Not dying; imperishable; unending; immortal; as, the undyingsouls of men.
To stop; to close; to make fast; as, to instop the seams.[Obs.] Dryden.
